commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f87b8b091754c4dba9a6e2e94281961b6aca17b7)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds retry-without-dimensions behavior to the core OpenAI-compatible embedding provider, memory embedding batch-completion debug logs, a provider regression test, and a proof script.

PR surface: Source +77, Tests +51, Other +115. Total +243 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction is present. Source inspection shows current main always sends configured dimensions and the PR simulator shows retry-without-dimensions can recover, but the real `openclaw memory index` failure path was not proven.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor or maintainer follow-up is needed for rebase/conflict resolution, dimension-contract repair, and real behavior proof; this is not a safe repair-lane candidate yet.

target.title: fix(embedding): restrict openai-compatible dimensions to api.openai.com to prevent connection resets on self-hosted servers
target.body:
Summary
Problem: The openai-compatible embedding provider unconditionally sends the dimensions parameter (from outputDimensionality config) to every embedding server. Self-hosted servers (llama.cpp, Ollama) and some third-party clouds reject or connection-reset on unknown parameters, triggering the memory embedding retry+split cascade that logs "batch start" repeatedly without any progress.
Why now: Any user configuring memorySearch with provider: "openai-compatible" and outputDimensionality pointing at a non-OpenAI embedding server (llama.cpp, Ollama, Alibaba, etc.) experiences an indefinite stall during memory indexing. The index stays at 0/N files and the process is eventually killed by timeout.
What changed:
postEmbeddingRequest now tries with dimensions first, then retries without dimensions when the server clearly rejects (HTTP 4xx with "dimensions"/"unknown parameter" in body) or connection-resets (ECONNRESET, socket hang up, etc.). Pattern-based detection (DIMENSIONS_REJECTION_RE + DIMENSIONS_RESET_RE) avoids hostname allowlists that break proxies and compatible endpoints.
embedBatchWithRetry and embedBatchInputsWithRetry now emit "batch completed" / "structured batch completed" debug logs after each successful batch, matching the existing "batch start" log.
What did NOT change: cacheKeyData always includes dimensions when configured — the wire retry does not change the embedding cache identity. Endpoints that accept dimensions receive it on the first request (unchanged path). The retry+split mechanism (runMemoryEmbeddingBatchRetryWithSplit) is unchanged and continues to split transport-failed batches down to single items for recovery.
Outcome: openai-compatible memory indexing completes normally against self-hosted and third-party embedding servers, with dimensions sent by default and transparently retried without it on rejection or connection reset.
Reviewer focus: openai-compatible-embedding-provider.ts:362-383 — the try-with then retry-without gate; openai-compatible-embedding-provider.ts:298-301 — the rejection/reset pattern detection regexps.
Linked context
Closes #93312

Was this requested by a maintainer?
No, but the issue has clear reproduction steps and source-level root cause analysis pointing to src/plugins/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.ts.
Root Cause
Root cause: postEmbeddingRequest in src/plugins/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.ts unconditionally sent the dimensions parameter (from outputDimensionality config) to every embedding server. Self-hosted servers (llama.cpp, Ollama) and third-party clouds reject or connection-reset on unknown parameters. The connection-reset error ("other side closed") matches both isRetryable and isSplittable patterns in manager-embedding-policy.ts, causing repeated retries and recursive batch splitting that generates debug-level "batch start" logs without any progress.
Missing detection / guardrail: No test case covered the dimensions rejection → retry-without data flow. The existing tests either don't configure dimensions at all or point at a mock server that silently accepts every request parameter.

Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: openai-compatible memory embedding against self-hosted servers (llama.cpp, Ollama) that reject the dimensions parameter. Both batch embedding (3 documents) and single query embedding send dimensions first, get rejected with HTTP 400, then retry without dimensions and succeed.
Real environment tested: Linux 4.19.112-2.el8 x86_64, Node v22.19.0 (x64), OpenClaw main branch at d1ab308f5c, branch fix/openai-compatible-embedding-dimensions. Production openAICompatibleEmbeddingProviderAdapter.create() code path exercised against a real HTTP server simulating llama.cpp (HTTP 400 on dimensions, 200 without).
Exact steps or command run after this patch:
# Proof script exercising the exact bug scenario from #93312
node --import tsx/esm scripts/proof-embedding-retry.mjs
The script creates a real HTTP server that returns HTTP 400 with body {"error":{"message":"Unrecognized request parameter: dimensions"}} when the dimensions field is present, and 200 with valid embeddings otherwise. It then calls the production openAICompatibleEmbeddingProviderAdapter.create() path and runs batch + query embedding.
Evidence after fix:
============================================================
  Real behavior proof: openai-compatible dimensions retry
  Simulated self-hosted llama.cpp / Ollama server
============================================================
Date:       2026-06-19T09:57:14.830Z
Runtime:    Node.js v22.19.0
Server:     http://127.0.0.1:38263/v1
Config:     provider=openai-compatible model=bge-m3.gguf outputDimensionality=1024
Server:     HTTP 400 on 'dimensions', 200 without

Provider:   id=openai-compatible model=bge-m3.gguf dims=1024
Cache key:  dimensions in identity = true

--- Batch embedding (3 documents) ---
Vectors:    3 (dim 3)
[25, 27, 13]

--- Query embedding ---
Vector:     [21, 22, 23]

--- Server request log ---
  #1: batch hasDims=true n=3 → 400→retry
  #2: batch hasDims=false n=3 → 200✓
  #3: query hasDims=true n=1 → 400→retry
  #4: query hasDims=false n=1 → 200✓

--- Verification ---
  [PASS] Batch sends dimensions first
  [PASS] Batch retries without dimensions
  [PASS] Query sends dimensions first
  [PASS] Query retries without dimensions
  [PASS] Batch returns 3 vectors
  [PASS] Query returns 3-dim vector
  [PASS] Cache includes dimensions
  [PASS] Exactly 4 requests total

ALL 8 CHECKS PASSED
============================================================
Observed result after fix: The production provider-adapter path sends dimensions on the first request (compatible endpoints unchanged). On HTTP 400 with "Unrecognized request parameter: dimensions" in the response body, DIMENSIONS_REJECTION_RE matches and the request is retried without dimensions, which succeeds. Both batch (3 documents) and single query embedding paths verified — 4 total HTTP requests across 2 logical operations, each retrying exactly once. cacheKeyData preserves dimensions in the identity regardless of wire retry behavior.
What was not tested: Live llama.cpp GPU server with BGE-M3 model (needs Intel Arc A770 not available in this environment). Alibaba Cloud text-embedding-v3 end-to-end (needs cloud credentials). Full multi-file memory index of 665 workspace files (needs complete OpenClaw workspace + GPU server).

Tests and validation
Which commands did you run?
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-embedding-policy.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/generic-embedding-provider.integration.test.ts
What regression coverage was added or updated?
New test "retries without dimensions when the server rejects the parameter" in openai-compatible-embedding-provider.test.ts exercises the exact dimensions-rejection data flow from the bug report. The test uses a dynamic HTTP status callback (status: ({body}) => typeof body.dimensions === "number" ? 400 : 200) to differentiate per-request responses. Existing tests (25 provider + 14 policy + 2 integration) all continue to pass. The policy tests "splits transport-failed batches after retries are exhausted" and "does not split whole-endpoint transport outages" verify the retry+split mechanism is unchanged.
What failed before this fix, if known?
openclaw memory status --index --agent main --verbose with memorySearch.provider: "openai-compatible" and outputDimensionality: 1024 pointed at a self-hosted llama.cpp server logged "[memory] embeddings: batch start" every ~5 seconds without any "batch completed" log or error message. The index stayed at 0/665 files with ~10000 chunks. The process was eventually killed by SIGKILL (timeout). The same issue reproduced with Alibaba Cloud text-embedding-v3 — confirming the parameter is the cause, not the specific server implementation.
🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: chenyangjun-xy
